channel.title: ML Theory on Mateo Juliani
channel.description: Recent content in ML Theory on Mateo Juliani

item.title: But Where Does L2 Regularization Come From?
item.description: I learned about L2 / L1 regularization early on in my machine learning career as one of those empirical “tricks” one could use to prevent overfitting. The logic went as follows - by adding the sum of the squared (or absolute) value of the weights, you could prevent the model from learning large values for weights, which would prevent the network from learning weights that could perfectly model (overfit to) the data. Indeed, several blogs (such as this one by Google) provide a similar argument to the one above.
